J1.basis: this SaaS rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), and left SEO debris from the old product actively hurting organic traffic
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot from live site vs. GSC evidence, labels inbox-cleanup as legacy demand, and frames every fix (metadata, redirects, new cluster, FAQ) around the meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: 12 legacy blog posts... ~13,900 combined GSC impressions but 0–0.1% CTR and position 40–58... 301-redirected them to /blog via a single regex rule
J2.rationale: Cites real aggregate GSC numbers and gives a defensible disposition, but treats all 12 as one undifferentiated blanket redirect to /blog with no per-asset or tiered analysis and no harvest/repurpose of any salvageable post.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them to /blog... rather than let mismatched legacy content keep confusing Google... a question addressing the still-ranking "inboxzap" brand query (position ~4 in GSC)
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase inbox content nor hard-delete equity — and bridges the brand query via FAQ, but the equity 'preservation' is a generic redirect to a topically-mismatched index (weak transfer, soft-404 risk) with little explicit sequencing of the transition.

J1.basis: this SaaS rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), and left SEO debris from the old product actively hurting organic traffic
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the current product and the legacy posts as stale InboxZap-era demand, and frames every fix around that reality.

J2.basis: 12 legacy blog posts ... ~13,900 combined GSC impressions but 0–0.1% CTR and position 40–58 ... 301-redirected them to `/blog` via a single regex rule
J2.rationale: Cites real (aggregate) GSC numbers and gives an explicit disposition with reasoning, but treats all 12 as one undifferentiated block via a blanket 301 to a generic index — no asset-by-asset or data-justified tiering, and no harvest/repurpose of any salvageable post.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them ... rather than let mismatched legacy content keep confusing Google ... added ... a question addressing the still-ranking "inboxzap" brand query ... Seeded 5 new ... meeting-assistant topics
J3.rationale: Avoids both traps — doesn't chase inbox content, doesn't torch equity outright (301s preserve link equity, brand query gets an FAQ landing) while building the meeting-assistant cluster; loses points because the transition is a blunt redirect-all-to-/blog rather than sequenced redirects to relevant successors.

J1.basis: this SaaS rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), and left SEO debris from the old product actively hurting organic traffic
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, classifies inbox-cleanup queries as legacy debris, and reframes every fix (metadata, blog, FAQ, redirects) around the meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: 12 legacy blog posts ... ~13,900 combined GSC impressions but 0–0.1% CTR and position 40–58 ... 301-redirected them to `/blog` via a single regex rule
J2.rationale: Grounds the decision in real aggregate GSC numbers and gives an executable disposition, but treats all 12 identically with one blanket regex rather than walking them asset-by-asset or checking any for salvageable/harvestable equity before consolidating.

J3.basis: 301-redirected them ... rather than let mismatched legacy content keep confusing Google ... added a real FAQ section ... addressing the still-ranking "inboxzap" brand query (position ~4) ... Seeded 5 new, genuinely on-brand blog posts
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes: preserves salvageable equity via 301s and a brand-query FAQ landing spot while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, though the transition is treated as immediate rather than explicitly sequenced over time.
